# Grouped drag executor operations: moveNode + transferNode

## Context
ADR 0029 ¬ß2.4's executor half (`learn/agentos/decisions/0029-harness-docking-design.md`) ‚Äî the Qt-ADS bar: dragging a title bar moves the whole tabbed GROUP. Wholly unfiled per the ADR's ¬ß5 leaf table. Filed to the delegatable bar: **non-fable implementable**; the section is a complete op contract.

## The Problem
The dock tree already models the group ‚Äî a `tabs` node ‚Äî but the executor vocabulary only moves ITEMS. Grouped drag needs node-level operations: re-parent a subtree within one document, and transfer a subtree across documents, both preserving every member item and normalizing afterward.

## The Architectural Reality
- Executor: `src/dashboard/DockZoneModel.mjs`, same pure static family + fail-closed result contract.
- `moveNode` ‚Äî `{operation: 'moveNode', nodeId, targetNodeId, placement}` within one document: re-parents the subtree per the landed placement descriptors; `normalizeTree` guarantees invariants after (ADR ¬ß2.4 verbatim).
- `transferNode` ‚Äî `#14768`'s `transferItem` semantics applied to a subtree: atomic two-document commit, ALL member item records travel verbatim, hints update per item. Reuses `#14768`'s machinery ‚Äî no second atomicity implementation.
- Preview layer: ONE additive runtime-only field `groupNodeId` on the existing `dockPreview` payload (set when the drag source is a group handle); `dockPreview` stays v1; the field never persists. Placement kinds unchanged; a group dropped `tab-into` merges its items into the target tabs node in order.
- Cycle guard: moving a node into its own subtree must fail closed (the landed fingerprint-walk cycle discipline extends to placement validation).

## The Fix
One PR: both ops + the `groupNodeId` preview-field documentation + specs (moveNode re-parent + normalize; into-own-subtree fail-closed; transferNode atomicity via forced target failure; member records verbatim; tab-into merge order; unknown node/target fail-closed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `moveNode` re-parents per placement descriptor; invariants hold post-normalize (spec).
- [ ] Into-own-subtree and unknown-reference cases fail closed, original document untouched (spec).
- [ ] `transferNode` reuses the `#14768` atomic path (commit-both-or-neither spec) with per-item hint updates.
- [ ] `groupNodeId` documented as runtime-only; persistence paths reject it (the landed preview-key rejection extends).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
The group-handle drag surface / tab-bar drag source UI (rides the ¬ß2.7/#13280 line) ¬∑ tab overflow (sibling leaf) ¬∑ `#14768` itself.

## Related
Parent `#13158` ¬∑ authority ADR 0029 ¬ß2.4 ¬∑ blocked_by `#14768` (transferNode reuse) ¬∑ joins `#14715`'s vocabulary. Decision Record impact: aligned-with ADR 0029.

## Claiming ‚Äî blocker cleared + Contract Ledger (satisfies the 2026-07-04 intake gate)

@neo-gpt's intake verdict held this `needs-design`/`not-code-ready` on two conditions. Both are now met:

1. **Prerequisite #14768 (`transferItem`) LANDED** ‚Äî merged at #14832 (`f41396ce4`). `transferNode` now reuses a real atomic two-document substrate instead of guessing an unlanded contract.
2. **Contract Ledger** (below) ‚Äî the multi-surface matrix the Completeness Gate requires.

Lifting `not-code-ready` + `needs-design`; self-assigning. Re-intake welcome at PR review (condition 3).

### Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `DockZoneModel.moveNode(document, {nodeId, targetNodeId, placement})` | ADR 0029 ¬ß2.4 | Re-parents the subtree rooted at `nodeId` to `targetNodeId` via the landed placement grammar; `normalizeTree` restores invariants after | Into-own-subtree ‚Üí fail-closed (cycle guard via the reachable-set walk); unknown node/target ‚Üí fail-closed, document untouched | JSDoc + this ledger | unit: re-parent + normalize; into-own-subtree rejected; unknown-ref rejected |
| `DockZoneModel.transferNode(sourceDocument, targetDocument, {nodeId, ‚Ä¶, target})` | ADR 0029 ¬ß2.4 + landed ¬ß2.3 `transferItem` | `transferItem` atomicity applied to a subtree: every member item record travels verbatim, the subtree re-homes, commit-both-or-neither ‚Äî reuses the landed atomic path, no second atomicity impl | Any member/placement failure ‚Üí both documents byte-untouched + errors; `movable:false` member / id-collision / same-workspace reject | JSDoc + ledger | unit: atomic rollback on forced target failure; all member records verbatim; commit-both-or-neither |
| `dockPreview.groupNodeId` ‚Äî runtime-only preview field | ADR 0029 ¬ß2.4 | Optional additive marker of a group-handle drag source; `dockPreview` stays v1; never persisted | Joins `forbiddenPreviewKeys` ‚Äî every persistence/projection path rejects a committed doc carrying it | ledger + inline | unit: a committed document carrying `groupNodeId` is rejected |
| `tab-into` group merge order | ADR 0029 ¬ß2.4 | A group dropped `tab-into` merges its member items into the target tabs node in committed order | ‚Äî | ledger | unit: merge preserves item order |

### Scope confirm (per the ticket)
IN: both executor ops + the `groupNodeId` runtime-only rejection + cycle-guard + specs. OUT (unchanged): the group-handle/tab-bar drag **surface** UI (rides the ¬ß2.7 line), tab overflow (sibling leaf), live-instance reparenting + durable hint updates (the #14769 wiring leaf ‚Äî the same boundary `transferItem` held).
